description: A vibrant reef slopes to 30 meters with healthy hard corals and frequent sightings of wobbegong sharks. Strong currents attract schooling barracuda and trevally, while pygmy seahorses hide in the soft corals. Best dived during slack tide for optimal conditions.

name: Warna Berwarna
region: Asia, Indonesia, West Papua, Raja Ampat Islands, Misool Island, Daram Island
description: This vibrant dive spot near Daram Island dazzles with colorful soft corals covering dramatic underwater ridges. Schools of batfish and barracuda patrol the steep walls while pygmy seahorses hide in the coral. Strong currents bring nutrient-rich waters, attracting manta rays and reef sharks. The site's pinnacles rise from 30 meters to just below the surface, offering thrilling drift dives. Visibility often exceeds 20 meters, revealing the full spectrum of Raja Ampat's marine biodiversity. Night dives showcase bioluminescent plankton and hunting octopuses.
